name: Vermont Maple Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, combine flour, sugars, baking powder, and salt. Mix until well blended.
2. In another bowl, combine oil, egg yolks, water, and maple flavoring. Turn the mixer to medium speed. Gradually add the liquid ingredients to the dry ingredients. Beat for 1 minute.
3. In a large metal bowl (not plastic), beat egg whites until foamy. Add cream of tartar. Whip at high speed until stiff peaks form.
4. Pour the batter over the egg whites. Fold gently until blended.
5. Fold in 1 cup of chopped walnuts.
6. Pour into an ungreased 10-inch angel food pan.
7. Bake at 325°F for 55 minutes. Increase the oven temperature to 350°F and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top springs back when touched.
8. Cool upside down. Remove from the pan.
9. In a medium saucepan, bring maple syrup to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer for 15 minutes, or until almost reduced to half.
10. Drizzle hot syrup over the cooled cake. Sprinkle with toasted walnuts.
11. Decorate with whipped cream (if desired).
